name: The Weeping Depths
subtitle: The sea remembers everything it swallows
theme: Perseverance / Hidden truths / The cost of power

shard.name: Seal Fragment of Wind
shard.description: A swirling green-gold crystal that moves like trapped breath. It whispers â€” not words, just the impression of them.

scenes.narration: The Shadow Reach is where Valdris's power is absolute â€” perpetual half-dark, where even shadows cast shadows. The air tastes like the moment before a storm that never arrives.
A voice fills the air â€” not through sound, but through the space between thoughts. No echo. No direction. Everywhere at once.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: Four Seal Fragments reclaimed. I confess I did not expect you to reach this gate intact. The Oracle made a better choice than I gave her credit for.

dialogue.speaker: Rei
dialogue.text: You speak to distract. It will not work.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: No. I speak because I am curious. A warden who has carried karmic weight for two thousand years â€” every demon you devoured left a scar, and you kept going. That is either extraordinary will or extraordinary self-destruction. I have not decided which.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: The funeral director. You have been around death long enough to know it does not frighten you â€” but you have never truly faced the possibility that all that poetry and laughter might simply... stop. Have you?

dialogue.speaker: Tao
dialogue.text: I provide a service to those experiencing a natural transition. There is a difference between my death and the ones I manage.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: Is there? You are very convincing. You almost convinced yourself.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: The dancer with the water that answers her. The princess who carries the weight of a name larger than herself. I know all four of you better than you know each other. I have been patient. I have had time.

dialogue.speaker: Lulu
dialogue.text: You spoke to me in the water. You tried to make hopelessness feel reasonable. It did not work.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: No. But it planted something. It always does. You will feel it in the gate â€” when the Fallen Angel comes at you and you wonder, just for a moment, if breaking beautiful things is the only honest act left in this world.

dialogue.speaker: Aya
dialogue.text: Then we will answer that question by not breaking. Move. Together.

scenes.narration: The voice pulls back â€” not gone, just watching from a greater distance. Lulu lets out a slow, controlled breath.

dialogue.speaker: Lulu
dialogue.text: He knew things. Specific things. Not just who we are â€” how we think.

dialogue.speaker: Rei
dialogue.text: I have carried karma for two thousand years. I have been afraid before. I fought anyway. That is the only answer that matters.

dialogue.speaker: Tao
dialogue.text: ...Knowing someone and stopping them are very different problems. He knows us. We are about to stop him. Advantage: ours.

dialogue.speaker: Aya
dialogue.text: Well said. Both of you.

chapters.title: The Great Sunken Temple
chapters.map_hint: Descend through the Sunken Temple — the Kraken and the Wind Fragment wait in the depths.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Shadow Reach gives way to the drowned labyrinth of the Great Sunken Temple — corridors held up by nothing but old magic, shadow-creatures patrolling every passage.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Rei
pre_dialogue.text: The Fallen Angel is ahead. She was something sacred once. The shadow takes what is strong and breaks it into tools.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Aya
pre_dialogue.text: Then we undo what he made of her. One way or another.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Tao
pre_dialogue.text: Careful through the corridors. Whatever is in here does not want us reaching the bottom.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The deepest chamber opens before them. The water is still here — ancient, black, lit from below by the pulse of the Wind Seal Fragment. And beneath the surface, something immense circles.

post_dialogue.speaker: Lulu
post_dialogue.text: The water here is grief-soaked. Something ancient was bound to this place against its will. I can feel it.

post_dialogue.speaker: Rei
post_dialogue.text: The Kraken. Pre-dates Valdris. He corrupted it when he broke the Seal — turned a guardian into a jailer.

post_dialogue.speaker: Aya
post_dialogue.text: Then we free it. That is what we do. Together.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Kraken rises from the black water — ancient, immense, its body threaded through with shadow-corruption that pulses in time with the Wind Seal Fragment. It does not want to be here. It has no choice.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Rei
pre_dialogue.text: It was a guardian once. This temple, these depths — they were its charge. Valdris broke the Seal and broke the creature with it.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Lulu
pre_dialogue.text: I can feel what it feels. It is not anger. It is grief. We end this for its sake.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Tao
pre_dialogue.text: For its sake, and for ours. Together.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Kraken falls. As its shadow-form dissolves, the Wind Seal Fragment rises from the depths — clear and cold, lit with the memory of open air.

post_dialogue.speaker: Rei
post_dialogue.text: The karma lifts. It can finally rest. After so long.

post_dialogue.speaker: Lulu
post_dialogue.text: I am glad it is free. I just wish there had been another way.

post_dialogue.speaker: Aya
post_dialogue.text: Sometimes the only kindness left is the one that ends suffering. It is not comfortable. But it is true.

post_dialogue.speaker: Tao
post_dialogue.text: Four Fragments. One more. And then we face him directly.

dialogue.text: As the water stills, a figure steps from the flooded alcove at the chamber's edge — draped in gold and ancient authority, armor marked with a crest that predates every kingdom any of them have heard of.

dialogue.speaker: Rex
dialogue.text: I have been held in these depths longer than I can account for. The moment the guardian fell — a chain snapped. I could breathe again. I am Rex, a king from an age before this war. I will not stand still while darkness finishes what it started.

dialogue.speaker: Aya
dialogue.text: An immortal king, kept prisoner in a sunken temple. You have borne witness to ages. Why stand with us now?

dialogue.speaker: Rex
dialogue.text: Because you are the first ones in two centuries who came here to free something rather than take something. I would follow that.

dialogue.speaker: Tao
dialogue.text: From warden to dragoon to immortal king. We gather well.

dialogue.speaker: Rei
dialogue.text: Six. The number holds weight.

dialogue.speaker: Valka
dialogue.text: Then we are complete. What comes next — we face it together.
